titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 675

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=68 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-02 13:02:56.779574+00 | translation moa=67 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-02 13:02:56.801876+00

p.cl: Thomas Prior et Conuentus dunelmensis Ecclesie . omnibus ad quos presens scriptum peruenerit  salutem in domino.

Sciatis quod cum inspexissem Cartas domine Ede de paxtona
et Roberti primogeniti sui et Roberti et Willelmi filiorum suorum clericorum et filiarum suarum Cecilie et Matildis et Ogeri de Tyeys clerici nostri in modum Cyrographi confectas
et sigillorum suorum apposicione confirmatas per quas concesserunt et tradiderunt eidem clerico nostro Ogero de Tyeys . omnes piscarias suas de paxtona super twede cum
omnibus libertatibus . asiamentis et omnibus aliis sine aliquo retinemento ad easdem piscarias pertinentibus habend’ et tenend’ eidem ogero et heredibus suis uel
assignatis . a festo sancti martini in hyeme . anno domini . m . cc . tricesimo sexto usque ad terminum nouem annorum subsequentum completum. Nos donacionem et con
cessionem inter prefatas Edam et Robertum primogenitum . et Robertum et Willelmum filios suos clericos . et Ceciliam et matildam filias suas et dictum ogerum de tyeys
factas super prescriptis piscariis de paxtona plurimum approbantes  eas prout in cartis suprascriptis plenius contintetur duximus confirmandas et presentis scripti
cui est nostrum sigillum appensum patrocinio fecimus communiri . Saluis custodiis ecclesiis dunelmi et de Coldinghami contingentibus .
Thomas the Prior, and the Convent of the church of Durham, to all to whom the present written deed shall come : greeting in the Lord.

Know that since I have examined the Charters of the lady Ada of Paxton, Robert her own first-born, Robert and William her sons, clerks, her own daughters, Cecilia and Matilda, and of Ogier of Tyas our clerk, prepared in the form of a Cyrograph, and confirmed by the application of their seals, by which they had granted and handed over to our same clerk, Ogier of Tyas, all their fisheries in Paxton upon the Tweed with all liberties, easements, and all other things pertaining to the same fisheries, without any withholding, for the same Ogier and his heirs or assignees to have and to hold, from the feast of Saint Martin in the winter, in the 1236th year of the Lord, until the term of nine years following is completed : We, very much approving the donation and grant between the aforementioned Ada, Robert the first-born, Robert and William her sons, the clerks, Cecilia and Matilda her daughters, and the said Ogier de Tyas, made concerning the fisheries of Paxton described before, have considered them to be confirmed exactly as it is the more fully contained in the charters described above; and we have caused them to be reinforced by the protection of the present written deed to which is appended our seal. Saving the wardships contingent on the churches of Durham and of Coldingham.
